title: Pitchfork Configuration
description:
Configuration schema for pitchfork.toml daemon supervisor configuration files.

Note: When read from a file, daemon keys are short names (e.g., "api").
After merging, keys become qualified DaemonIds (e.g., "project/api").

daemons.description: Map of daemon IDs to their configurations

namespace.description:
Optional explicit namespace declared in this file.

This applies to per-file read/write flows. Merged configs may contain
daemons from multiple namespaces and leave this as `None`.

settings.description:
Settings configuration (merged from all config files).

**Note:** This field exists for serialization round-trips and for
`PitchforkToml::merge()` to collect per-file overrides.  It is **not**
consumed by the global `settings()` singleton, which is populated
independently by `Settings::load()` to avoid a circular dependency
between `PitchforkToml` and `Settings`.  Do not rely on mutations to
this field being reflected in `settings()`.

CpuLimit.description: CPU usage limit as a percentage (e.g. 80 for 80% of one core, 200 for 2 cores)

oneOf.description: Retrigger only if the previous run has finished (success or error)
Always retrigger, stopping the previous run if still active
Retrigger only if the previous run succeeded
Retrigger only if the previous run failed

DaemonId.description: Daemon name (e.g. 'api') or qualified ID ('namespace/name') for cross-namespace references

MemoryLimit.description: A byte size, as either a human-readable string (e.g. "1.5 KiB") or a number of bytes

OnOutputHook.description:
Output hook configuration.

Accepts two forms:
```toml
on_output = "echo matched"                              # shorthand (run only)
on_output = { run = "echo matched", filter = "ready" }  # full
```

Pattern matching (`filter` / `regex`) is performed against ANSI-stripped
output and covers both stdout and stderr.

debounce.description:
Minimum time between successive firings (humantime, e.g. `"500ms"`).
Defaults to `"1000ms"`.

filter.description: Fire when a line of output contains this substring

regex.description: Fire when a line of output matches this regular expression

run.description: Command to run when the output condition is met

PitchforkTomlCron.description: Cron scheduling: a cron expression string, or { schedule, retrigger, immediate } object

immediate.description: Trigger immediately on first check (default: false)

PitchforkTomlDaemon.description: Configuration for a single daemon (internal representation with DaemonId)

archive_hook.description:
Archive hook command invoked before retention prunes this daemon's logs.
Overrides the global `settings.logs.archive_hook.command` when set.

auto.description: Automatic start/stop behavior based on shell hooks

boot_start.description: Whether to start this daemon automatically on system boot

cpu_limit.description:
CPU usage limit as a percentage (e.g. 80 for 80%, 200 for 2 cores).
The supervisor periodically monitors CPU usage and kills the process if it exceeds the limit.

cron.description: Cron scheduling configuration for periodic execution

depends.description: List of daemon IDs that must be started before this one

dir.description: Working directory for the daemon. Relative paths are resolved from the pitchfork.toml location.

env.description: Environment variables to set for the daemon process

line_retention.description:
Maximum number of log entries to keep per daemon.
Overrides the global `settings.logs.line_retention` when set.

memory_limit.description:
Memory limit for the daemon process (e.g. "50MB", "1GiB").
The supervisor periodically monitors RSS and kills the process if it exceeds the limit.

mise.description:
Wrap this daemon's command with `mise x --` for tool/env setup.
Overrides the global `settings.general.mise` when set.

port.description: Port configuration: expected ports and auto-bump settings

pty.description: Allocate a pseudo-terminal for the daemon process.

ready_cmd.description: Shell command to poll for readiness (exit code 0 = ready)

ready_delay.description: Delay in seconds before considering the daemon ready

ready_http.description: HTTP URL to poll for readiness. Accepts any 2xx response by default, or configured statuses.

ready_output.description: Regex pattern to match in ANSI-stripped stdout/stderr to determine readiness

ready_port.description:
TCP port to check for readiness (connection success = ready).
Accepts a port number or a Tera template string that renders to one.

retry.description:
Number of times to retry if the daemon fails.
Can be a number (e.g., `3`) or `true` for infinite retries.

run.description: The command to run. Prepend with 'exec' to avoid shell process overhead.

stop_signal.description:
Stop signal and optional per-daemon timeout. Accepts a signal name string
or `{ signal = "...", timeout = "..." }` object.

time_retention.description:
Maximum age of log entries to keep (e.g. "7d", "30d").
Overrides the global `settings.logs.time_retention` when set.

user.description: Unix user to run this daemon as. Overrides `settings.supervisor.user` when set.

watch.description: File patterns to watch for changes

watch_mode.description:
File watching backend mode.

- `native`: use platform-native notifications (default)
- `poll`: use polling-based watcher
- `auto`: prefer native, fall back to polling if native watch fails

PitchforkTomlDaemonLogs.description:
Per-daemon log configuration sub-table `[daemons.<name>.logs]`.

Fields here override the top-level daemon fields (`time_retention`,
`line_retention`, `archive_hook`) and the global `[settings.logs]` defaults.

archive_hook.description: Archive hook command invoked before retention prunes this daemon's logs.

line_retention.description: Maximum number of log entries to keep per daemon.

log_format.description:
Log line format: `json`, `logfmt`, or `text`.
Defaults to `text` (no parsing).

time_retention.description: Maximum age of log entries to keep (e.g. "7d", "30d").

on_exit.description: Command to run on any daemon termination (clean exit, crash, or stop)

on_fail.description: Command to run when the daemon fails and all retries are exhausted

on_output.description: Hook triggered when the daemon produces matching output

on_ready.description: Command to run when the daemon becomes ready

on_retry.description: Command to run before each retry attempt

on_stop.description: Command to run when the daemon is explicitly stopped by pitchfork

PortBump.description: Port bump: true = unlimited, false/0 = disabled, number = max attempts

PortConfig.description: Port config: a port number, array of ports, or { expect, bump } object

ReadyHttp.description: HTTP readiness check: a URL string accepting any 2xx response, or { url, status } object with exact accepted status codes

oneOf.description: HTTP URL to poll for readiness; any 2xx response is ready

status.description: Exact HTTP status codes that indicate readiness. Omit to accept any 2xx response.

url.description: HTTP URL to poll for readiness

ReadyPort.description: TCP readiness port: a port number, or a template string rendering to one (e.g. "{{ daemons.redis.port }}")

oneOf.description: Tera template that renders to a port number

Retry.description: Retry: true = indefinite, false/0 = none, number = count

auto_start.description: Automatically start the standalone API server

bind_address.description: IP address the API server binds to

bind_port.description: Port the standalone API server listens on

port_attempts.description: Number of consecutive ports to try if api.bind_port is in use

token.description: Authentication token for API access when bound to non-loopback addresses

autostop_delay.description: Delay before auto-stopping daemons when leaving a directory

log_file_level.description: File log level (trace, debug, info, warn, error)

log_level.description: Console log level (trace, debug, info, warn, error)

mise.description: Wrap daemon commands with mise x -- globally

mise_bin.description: Explicit path to the mise binary

shell.description: Shell command used to execute daemon run scripts

startup_log_timestamps.description: Show timestamps in startup log output

rate_limit.description: Maximum IPC requests per second per connection

batch_size.description: Maximum log entries per archive hook invocation

command.description: Command to run before retention deletes old log entries

log_format.description: Default log format for daemons (json | logfmt | text)

timestamp_format.description: strftime format for log timestamps in `pitchfork logs` output

auto_start.description: Automatically start daemons when accessed via proxy URL

auto_start_timeout.description: Maximum time to wait for an auto-started daemon to become ready

auto_trust.description: Automatically install the proxy TLS certificate into the system trust store

enable.description: Enable the reverse proxy server for daemons

host.description: Bind address for the reverse proxy server

https.description: Enable HTTPS for the reverse proxy

lan.description: Enable LAN mode for the reverse proxy

lan_ip.description: Pin a specific LAN IP address instead of auto-detecting

port.description: Port the reverse proxy server listens on

tld.description: Top-level domain used for proxy URLs

tls_cert.description: Path to TLS certificate file (PEM format) for HTTPS proxy

tls_key.description: Path to TLS private key file (PEM format) for HTTPS proxy

wildcard.description: Enable wildcard subdomain matching for proxy routes

worktree.description: Enable git worktree / jj workspace auto-discovery for slug routing

cleanup_orphans.description: Kill orphaned daemon processes when supervisor starts

container.description: Enable container/PID1 mode for running inside Docker containers

cpu_violation_threshold.description: Consecutive CPU-over-limit samples before killing a daemon

port_bump_attempts.description: Maximum port increment attempts when auto_bump_port is enabled

ready_check_interval.description: Interval between ready checks (HTTP, TCP, command)

restart_delay.description: Delay between stop and start during restart

stop_timeout.description: Maximum time to wait for daemon to stop gracefully

user.description: Default user to run daemon processes as

stat_history.description: Number of stat samples to keep for graphs

auto_start.description: Automatically start web UI when supervisor starts

base_path.description: URL path prefix for the web UI (e.g. "ps" serves at /ps/)

log_lines.description: Initial number of log lines to display

port_attempts.description: Number of ports to try if default is in use

sse_poll_interval.description: Server-Sent Events poll interval for log streaming

StopConfig.description: Stop signal config: a signal name string, or { signal, timeout } object

WatchMode.description: File watch backend mode for daemon `watch` patterns.

oneOf.description: Use polling backend; more compatible on networked filesystems.
Prefer native backend, fall back to polling when native watch setup fails.
